classObjective: Graph linear inequalities in two variables written in slope-intercept and standard form and write linear inequalities to model real-world constraints.
workedExamples: 6-5, Example 1 — Graph Inequalities in Slope-Intercept Form
6-5, Example 2 — Graph Inequalities in Standard Form
6-5, Example 3 — Write and Graph Inequalities from Context
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: dashed vs. solid boundary lines; half-plane shading; test point method; writing inequalities from context
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice graphing two-variable inequalities in both slope-intercept and standard form
independentPractice: Practice problems graphing linear inequalities and writing inequalities to model real-world constraints
assessment: Exit ticket: write and graph an inequality for a budget of at most $200 for x items at $25 each and y items at $40 each
capReflection: How does the test point method help you determine which side of the boundary line to shade?
